title: Valid Parentheses
description:
Given a string `s` containing just the characters `(`, `)`, `{`, `}`,
`[` and `]`, determine if the input string is **valid**.

An input string is valid if:

1. Open brackets are closed by the same type of bracket.
2. Open brackets are closed in the correct order.
3. Every close bracket has a corresponding open bracket of the same type.

examples.explanation: The brackets are not closed in the correct order.

solutions.summary: Push opening brackets; pop and match when a closing bracket appears.

steps.title: Map closers to openers
steps.explanation: Set up a lookup from each closing bracket to its matching opening bracket, and an empty stack to track unmatched openers.

steps.title: Push openers, match closers
steps.explanation: Iterate the string. Opening brackets are pushed. For a closing bracket, the top of the stack must be its matching opener — otherwise the string is invalid.
